description: platformAutomerge is deliberately false: main requires the ci-pass status check, and true triggers the registration race (native auto-merge completes in the ~1s window before ci-pass registers, landing a red bump). false makes Renovate merge via its own run after re-confirming green.

customManagers.description: Makefile tool versions via inline # renovate: comments (PLANTUML_VERSION).

Air-gap image inventory: each image ref on the line below its # renovate: comment. currentValue is the TAG and currentDigest the optional @sha256 — captured separately so a digest-pinned row (distroless, whose `nonroot` tag MOVES and has no semver) is bump-able. Capturing them together made currentValue the bare 64-hex, which the docker datasource cannot version, so the row was skipped (skipReason invalid-value) and only the Dockerfile half of a digest bump ever landed. The sha256: prefix MUST stay INSIDE the group: the datasource emits newDigest as sha256:<hex>, so capturing bare hex yields a permanent currentDigest/newDigest mismatch that retries forever.

Consumed release/tool versions pinned in .env.example (Tekton, kubectl, tkn, argocd CLI+manifest, cloud-provider-kind, harbor chart). Only # renovate:-annotated lines are matched. The optional extractVersion + registryUrl groups mirror every annotation shape used (extractVersion for tkn's v-prefix strip, registryUrl for the harbor helm datasource). currentValue/currentDigest are split for the same reason as the images.txt manager above — DISTROLESS_STATIC_TAG is `nonroot@sha256:...`, and capturing it whole made it unversionable and therefore untracked, so images.txt would bump while this file silently did not and check-image-alignment's env arm would fail. Deliberately NO versioning group: the docker datasource's defaultVersioning is already `docker`, so `versioning=docker` in an annotation is inert AND would not match this pattern, silently untracking whatever it annotates.

Image tags in k8s + Tekton manifests, referenced as ${HARBOR_URL}/${HARBOR_INFRA_PROJECT}/<repo>:<tag> (a ${VAR}-prefixed ref the built-in docker manager can't parse). depName/currentValue are extracted from the ref so these bump in lockstep with images/images.txt; the check-image-alignment gate is the deterministic backstop.

packageRules.description: Wait 3 days before automerging major updates

Cluster-only platform tools (helm, kubectl, tkn, argocd, cloud-provider-kind, harbor chart, Tekton, Istio) are exercised ONLY by the local KinD e2e — CI runs offline gates, never a cluster (see CLAUDE.md verification-honesty). A breaking MAJOR would otherwise auto-merge green-but-blind (helm 3->4 did, 2026-07-08). Require manual Dependency-Dashboard approval for their majors so a human runs `make e2e-kind` first; minors/patches still automerge on green.
Group GitHub Actions into one PR
Group mise tool bumps + 3-day buffer so aqua/github-tags release artifacts publish before the PR opens

kubectl is pinned twice for one logical version — .mise.toml (mise manager, depName 'kubectl', the jump-box toolchain) and .env.example KUBECTL_VERSION (custom.regex, depName 'kubernetes/kubernetes', the air-gap fallback used by 00-install-prereqs.sh). They are DIFFERENT managers with DIFFERENT depNames, so by default Renovate opens two separate PRs that momentarily desync check-toolchain-alignment (the gate that asserts they stay equal). Group both depNames so the two pins bump in ONE PR. Must come AFTER the 'mise tools' rule so it overrides kubectl's group; keeps the 3-day buffer (kubernetes releases can tag before the release assets publish). (istio/pilot is also pinned in two files but with the SAME depName, so Renovate already bundles it — no group needed there.)

Group all docker-datasource images (Dockerfile ARG bases + images/images.txt inventory) into one PR so the same image pinned in multiple files bumps together. pinDigests off: it governs ADDING a digest to a ref that has none, NOT updating one that exists (renovate lookup: the currentDigest path never consults pinDigests), so the digest-pinned distroless row still bumps normally; these are build/mirror images for a demo, not a published runtime artifact. Do NOT add a {matchUpdateTypes:[digest], enabled:false} companion here: that guard exists for SEMVER-tagged images where a digest refresh can mask a version bump, and distroless is the documented opposite — `nonroot` MOVES and has no semver, so the digest refresh is the ONLY update path. Scoped to docker it would also kill digest updates for every app Dockerfile's digest-pinned base-image ARG.

kaniko's real image is gcr.io/kaniko-project/executor; the manifest customManager derives depName=kaniko-project/executor which defaults to Docker Hub (no such repo -> chronic 401 on the dashboard lookup). Point it at gcr.io. images.txt tracks the correct gcr.io ref and check-image-alignment is the deterministic backstop.

Gateway API CRDs are COUPLED to Istio: the CRD version must be one the pinned Istio vendors/tests against. A bot bumping it ALONE re-introduces the supportedFeatures []string->[]object skew that crash-loops controllers, or lands CRDs Istio does not understand. Group them so they move together and CI judges them as a unit.

HOLD gateway-api at <v1.6.0. It is NOT stale: v1.5.1 is the NEWEST version any Istio supports — Istio 1.30.2 (the latest release) vendors sigs.k8s.io/gateway-api v1.5.1, and CRDs newer than Istio's own Go client re-introduce the supportedFeatures []string->[]object skew that crash-loops controllers. Renovate solo-merged v1.6.0 once already. REMOVE THIS CAP the moment an Istio release vendors v1.6+; verify with: curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/istio/istio/release-<minor>/go.mod | grep gateway-api
